frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: brain as prediction machine driven by prediction errors across cognitive domains

frameworks.name: prediction-error coding
frameworks.one_line: mismatch between expected and realized events drives neural and cognitive processing

core_question: What are the generative mechanisms by which the brain forms, tests, and updates predictions across cognitive domains?
key_assumptions: prediction is a fundamental principle of neural computation
expectations are tested by matching them to realized events
prediction errors drive neural and cognitive processes and behavior
manipulations: comparison of confirmed vs violated predictions in subsequent processing
key_findings: multiple cognitive domains converge on prediction, preparation, anticipation, prospection, and expectation as central constructs
events that confirm predictions are processed differently from events that violate them
interpretations: predictive processing may be one fundamental principle of neural computation across cognition
no unified theory yet exists; open issues remain before predictive processing can be fully postulated for cognitive and neural function
limitations_noted: many open issues remain before a unified theory of predictive processing can be formulated
